disclaimer: Seed data compiled from public sources. Every row confirmed_by_counsel=false until ratified by immigration counsel. Not legal advice.

rules.notes: Statutory regular cap of 65,000 per fiscal year, in effect since FY2004 and unchanged through FY2027. Reached in the FY2027 initial registration period per USCIS 2026-03-31 announcement.

rules.source_citation: INA 214(g)(5)(C); H-1B Visa Reform Act of 2004
rules.notes: First 20,000 beneficiaries with a US master's degree or higher are exempt from the 65,000 regular cap (master's cap). Unchanged for FY2027; reached in the FY2027 initial selection.
6,800 of the 65,000 regular cap set aside for H-1B1 free-trade-agreement nationals (1,400 Chile, 5,400 Singapore); unused numbers roll into the following fiscal year's regular cap.

rules.source_citation: 84 FR 60307 (Registration Requirement for Petitioners Seeking To File H-1B Petitions on Behalf of Cap-Subject Aliens fee rule)
rules.notes: SUPERSEDED. $10 per-beneficiary registration fee, collected for the FY2021 (March 2020) through FY2025 (March 2024) registration seasons.

rules.source_citation: 89 FR 6194 (USCIS Fee Schedule final rule, Jan 31, 2024)
rules.notes: Non-refundable $215 per beneficiary per registration. Set by the April 2024 fee rule (89 FR 6194, rule effective 2024-04-01) but first collected for the FY2026 season; effective_date follows the seed convention of the first registration-period date the fee applied (FY2026 initial registration opened 2025-03-07 per USCIS alert). Charged again for the FY2027 season (March 4-19, 2026).

rules.source_citation: USCIS alert: FY 2027 H-1B Cap Initial Registration Period Opens on March 4
rules.notes: FY2027 initial registration opened at 12:00 noon Eastern on 2026-03-04 via USCIS online accounts.

rules.source_citation: USCIS alert: FY 2027 H-1B Cap Initial Registration Period Opens on March 4
rules.notes: CORRECTED: FY2027 initial registration closed at NOON Eastern on 2026-03-19, not 5:00 p.m. USCIS alert (release date 2026-01-30) states the period would 'open at noon Eastern on March 4 and run through noon Eastern on March 19, 2026.' Window dates March 4-19, 2026 confirmed from the primary USCIS page text.

rules.source_citation: USCIS alert: FY 2027 H-1B Initial Registration Selection Process Completed
rules.notes: USCIS announced 2026-03-31 that initial selections were complete; enough registrations for unique beneficiaries were received to reach both the regular cap and the master's cap. Selected registrants notified via myUSCIS accounts. Additional selection rounds possible later in FY if insufficient petitions are filed/approved.

rules.value: Computer-generated random selection among properly submitted registrations; since FY2025 season run on unique beneficiaries (beneficiary-centric) rather than registrations.
rules.notes: SUPERSEDED. Random lottery used from FY2021 (first electronic-registration season, March 2020) through the FY2026 season (March 2025). Replaced by the wage-weighted selection process effective 2026-02-27.

rules.value: Weighted selection: each unique beneficiary is entered into the selection pool once per OEWS wage level tier of the offered wage - Level IV = 4 entries, Level III = 3, Level II = 2, Level I = 1; offered wages below Level I receive 1 entry. Weighting uses the highest OEWS wage level that the offered wage equals or exceeds for the SOC code and area of intended employment.
rules.source_citation: Weighted Selection Process for Registrants and Petitioners Seeking To File Cap-Subject H-1B Petitions, final rule published 2025-12-29, effective 2026-02-27; amends 8 CFR 214.2(h)(8)(iii)
rules.notes: CONFIRMED effective 2026-02-27 and governed the FY2027 selection in March 2026: USCIS's 2026-01-30 alert states that if registrations exceed the cap 'we will conduct a weighted selection,' and the 2026-03-31 alert (which confirms the cap was oversubscribed) requires petitioners to submit 'evidence of the basis of the wage level selected on the registration.' First weighted lottery per Reddy Neumann (2026-07-01). Applies to both the regular-cap and master's-cap selections. In force as of 2026-07-06; no injunction or vacatur located.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(h)(8)(iii) as amended by weighted-selection final rule
rules.notes: Offered wage at or above OEWS Level IV: 4 entries in the selection pool.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(h)(8)(iii) as amended by weighted-selection final rule
rules.notes: Offered wage at or above OEWS Level III (but below Level IV): 3 entries.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(h)(8)(iii) as amended by weighted-selection final rule
rules.notes: Offered wage at or above OEWS Level II (but below Level III): 2 entries.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(h)(8)(iii) as amended by weighted-selection final rule
rules.notes: Offered wage at or above OEWS Level I (but below Level II): 1 entry.

rules.source_citation: Weighted-selection final rule preamble
rules.notes: Offered wages below OEWS Level I remain permissible where supported by an acceptable alternative wage source, but receive only a single entry.

rules.source_citation: Weighted-selection final rule; 8 CFR 214.2(h)(8)(iii)
rules.notes: CONFIRMED: beginning with the FY2027 season, each registration must include the SOC code, area of intended employment, and the highest OEWS wage level the offered wage meets or exceeds. Secondary confirmation: Greenberg Traurig alert (gtlaw.com, Feb 2026).

rules.value: In force as of 2026-07-06. No injunction, stay, or vacatur located; the rule governed the FY2027 lottery conducted in March 2026. Commentators anticipated APA/statutory-authority challenges, but no active case blocking the rule was identified as of early July 2026.
rules.source_citation: Reddy Neumann Brown analysis (2026-07-01); Fisher Phillips litigation state-of-play
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: absence-of-litigation is a negative claim sourced from reputable secondary sources (Reddy Neumann 2026-07-01; Fisher Phillips litigation state-of-play); no primary court docket confirmation. Independent re-search on 2026-07-06 likewise found no suit filed against the weighted-selection rule (the multistate and Chamber suits target the $100,000 proclamation, not this rule). Re-verify before each cap season.

rules.source_citation: 89 FR 7456 (Improving the H-1B Registration Selection Process final rule, effective 2024-03-04)
rules.notes: Selection is run on unique beneficiaries regardless of how many employers register them; each beneficiary must be identified by a valid passport or travel document. Remains in force alongside the 2026 weighted-selection rule.

rules.source_citation: USCIS alert: FY 2027 H-1B Initial Registration Selection Process Completed
rules.notes: Cap-subject petitions for FY2027 (including master's cap) may be filed beginning 2026-04-01, only for selected beneficiaries with valid registrations, at the location indicated in the selection notice.

rules.source_citation: Fragomen alert on FY2027 selection completion; USCIS selection notices
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: the 2026-06-30 end date appears only in secondary sources (Fragomen; practitioner blogs reporting selection notices stating a 4/1/2026-6/30/2026 filing window). The USCIS 2026-03-31 alert (retrieved directly 2026-07-06) says only that petitions must be filed 'within the filing period indicated on the relevant selection notice' and that the period 'will be at least 90 days'; it does not publish a universal end date. Treat 2026-06-30 as the typical notice deadline and check each selection notice.
Selected registrants receive a filing period of at least 90 days to submit the cap-subject I-129 petition.
Earliest permissible employment start date for FY2027 cap-subject petitions is 2026-10-01, the first day of fiscal year 2027. Confirmed by Reddy Neumann Brown (2026-07-01).

rules.source_citation: 89 FR 6194 (USCIS Fee Schedule final rule); USCIS G-1055 Fee Schedule
rules.notes: Form I-129 H-1B base filing fee (paper filing) for employers with 26 or more full-time-equivalent employees; ONLINE filing is $730 per the current G-1055 (edition 05/29/26, verified 2026-07-06: 'Paper Filing: $780 ... Online Filing: $730'). Employer-paid. Unchanged as of 2026-07-06; no new USCIS fee schedule rule located after the 2024 rule (only the premium processing adjustment).

rules.source_citation: 89 FR 6194 (USCIS Fee Schedule final rule); USCIS G-1055 Fee Schedule
rules.notes: Reduced I-129 base fee for small employers (25 or fewer FTE employees) and qualifying nonprofits.

rules.source_citation: INA 214(c)(9); H-1B Visa Reform Act of 2004
rules.notes: ACWIA education/training fee for employers with 25 or fewer FTE employees. Due with initial petitions and first extensions; exemptions for institutions of higher education, related/affiliated nonprofits, nonprofit and governmental research organizations, primary/secondary schools, and second-or-later extensions. Must be paid by the employer; cannot be passed to the worker.

rules.source_citation: INA 214(c)(9); H-1B Visa Reform Act of 2004
rules.notes: ACWIA fee for employers with 26 or more FTE employees. Same applicability and exemptions as the $750 tier.

rules.source_citation: INA 214(c)(12); H-1B Visa Reform Act of 2004
rules.notes: Required for initial H-1B grants and change-of-employer petitions; not required for extensions/amendments with the same employer. Employer-paid.

rules.source_citation: 89 FR 6194 (USCIS Fee Schedule final rule)
rules.notes: Asylum Program Fee payable with each I-129 by employers with 26 or more FTE employees. Applies to initial petitions, extensions, and amendments. Still in force as of 2026-07-06.

rules.source_citation: 89 FR 6194 (USCIS Fee Schedule final rule)
rules.notes: Reduced Asylum Program Fee for small employers (25 or fewer FTE employees).

rules.source_citation: 89 FR 6194 (USCIS Fee Schedule final rule)
rules.notes: Qualifying nonprofits are exempt from the Asylum Program Fee ($0).

rules.source_citation: Public Law 114-113, Div. O, Title IV, Sec. 402 (Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2016), as extended by Public Law 115-123, Sec. 30203 (Bipartisan Budget Act of 2018)
rules.notes: CORRECTED: the original 2025-09-30 sunset in Sec. 402 was EXTENDED to 2027-09-30 by the Bipartisan Budget Act of 2018 (Pub. L. 115-123, Sec. 30203). USCIS's page states verbatim: 'The fee is in effect until Sept. 30, 2027.' The current G-1055 Fee Schedule (edition 05/29/26) still lists the $4,000 Pub. L. 114-113 fee as payable when the Fraud Prevention and Detection fee is required, the employer has 50 or more US employees, and more than 50 percent are in H-1B, L-1A, or L-1B status. Fee remains in effect as of 2026-07-06.

rules.source_citation: 88 FR 89539 (2023 premium processing inflation adjustment)
rules.notes: SUPERSEDED. I-129 premium processing fee in effect 2024-02-26 through 2026-02-28.

rules.source_citation: Adjustment to Premium Processing Fees, final rule published 2026-01-12, effective 2026-03-01; USCIS Stabilization Act biennial CPI-U adjustment
rules.notes: CONFIRMED: I-129 premium processing (including H-1B) is $2,965 for requests postmarked on or after 2026-03-01. Same rule set I-140 premium at $2,965, certain I-539 at $2,075, certain I-765 at $1,780. Optional fee; payable by employer or on Form I-907.

rules.source_citation: USCIS Stabilization Act; 8 CFR 106.4
rules.notes: USCIS must take adjudicative action on premium-processed I-129 H-1B petitions within 15 business days or refund the fee.

rules.source_citation: Presidential Proclamation 'Restriction on Entry of Certain Nonimmigrant Workers' (2025-09-19), effective 12:01 a.m. EDT 2025-09-21; implemented by USCIS guidance of 2025-10-20
rules.notes: One-time $100,000 payment required as a condition for certain new H-1B petitions filed on or after 12:01 a.m. EDT 2025-09-21 for beneficiaries outside the US who do not hold a valid H-1B visa (consular or port-of-entry notification cases). Paid via pay.gov before filing; proof of payment must accompany the petition. Not an annual fee. IN EFFECT as of 2026-07-06, but under active litigation (see h1b-proclamation-100k-litigation-status).

rules.value: Applies to: (1) new H-1B petitions filed on or after 2025-09-21 for beneficiaries outside the US without a valid H-1B visa, including FY2027 cap-subject petitions requesting consular or port-of-entry notification; (2) petitions requesting change of status or extension where the beneficiary is found ineligible for that in-US action (petition treated as consular notification). Does NOT apply to: petitions filed before 2025-09-21; current H-1B visa holders or visa renewals on previously approved petitions; approved in-US amendment, change-of-status, change-of-employer, or extension petitions - including subsequent visa applications abroad based on those approved petitions.
rules.source_citation: USCIS, H-1B Specialty Occupations page, 'Presidential Proclamation on Restriction on Entry of Certain Nonimmigrant Workers' section (implementing the 2025-09-19 proclamation and 2025-10-20 guidance)
rules.notes: CONFIRMED from the USCIS primary page text (retrieved 2026-07-06): applies to petitions filed at/after 12:01 a.m. ET 2025-09-21 for beneficiaries outside the US without a valid H-1B visa, to petitions requesting consular notification, port-of-entry notification, or pre-flight inspection, and to change-of-status/extension/amendment petitions where USCIS finds the beneficiary ineligible for the in-US action. Does not apply to pre-2025-09-21 petitions or currently valid H-1B visa holders. An in-US amendment, change-of-status, or extension petition that is GRANTED is not subject, and the beneficiary may later apply for a visa abroad on that approved petition without triggering the fee. Payment via pay.gov before filing; petitions filed without proof of payment or an exception are denied.

rules.source_citation: USCIS, H-1B Specialty Occupations page, proclamation section (reflecting USCIS updated guidance of 2025-10-20)
rules.notes: CONFIRMED from the USCIS primary page text (retrieved 2026-07-06): the proclamation 'does not apply to a petition ... requesting an amendment, change of status, or extension of stay for an alien inside the United States where the alien is granted such amendment, change, or extension.' Key planning lever for FY2027 cap beneficiaries already in the US (e.g., F-1 students changing status). Caveat: if USCIS finds the beneficiary ineligible for the in-US action, the payment applies.

rules.value: Extraordinarily rare case-by-case exception by the Secretary of Homeland Security where the hire is in the national interest, no US worker is available, the beneficiary poses no security/welfare threat, and requiring the payment would significantly undermine US interests. Pre-approval requested by email to H1BExceptions@hq.dhs.gov with supporting evidence before filing. No exceptions publicly reported as granted as of 2026-03-31.
rules.source_citation: Proclamation of 2025-09-19, Sec. 1; USCIS, H-1B Specialty Occupations page, proclamation section ('Exceptions granted by the Secretary of Homeland Security')
rules.notes: Exception criteria and the H1BExceptions@hq.dhs.gov process CONFIRMED from the USCIS primary page text (retrieved 2026-07-06). UNVERIFIED: the claim that no exceptions had been granted as of 2026-03-31 rests solely on Fragomen reporting; no official grant/denial statistics are published.

rules.value: IN EFFECT BUT CONTESTED as of 2026-07-06. (1) D.D.C. upheld the proclamation on 2025-12-23 under INA 212(f) (Chamber of Commerce v. DHS, No. 1:25-cv-03675; plaintiffs appealed to the D.C. Circuit 2025-12-29). (2) D. Mass. (Judge Sorokin) vacated the $100,000 requirement on 2026-06-08 as an unconstitutional tax and APA violation (State of California v. Noem, No. 1:25-cv-13829, 20-state AG coalition filed 2025-12-12). (3) On 2026-06-12 the D. Mass. court itself temporarily stayed its vacatur, conditioned on the government moving for a stay in the First Circuit by 2026-06-18; the government filed that stay motion on 2026-06-18 (State of California v. Mullin, No. 26-1699 (1st Cir.)). The fee therefore remains collectible pending the First Circuit's ruling on the stay motion. Separate Global Nurse Force suit pending in N.D. Cal. (No. 3:25-cv-08454, filed 2025-10-03). Expect further changes; possible Supreme Court resolution.
rules.source_citation: D. Mass. decision 2026-06-08; stay order 2026-06-12; D.D.C. decision 2025-12-23
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: court docket entries not directly checked (PACER unavailable); sequence corroborated by consistent reporting from Fragomen, Vorys, Ogletree, Clark Hill, WR Immigration, and Gunster (June 2026). CORRECTED vs. prior draft: the 2026-06-12 stay was issued by the D. Mass. district court itself (temporary/administrative stay), not by the First Circuit; the government's First Circuit stay motion was filed 2026-06-18 and remained pending as of early July 2026. Operational takeaway unchanged: the $100,000 payment still applies to covered petitions as of 2026-07-06. Re-verify frequently.

rules.source_citation: USCIS alert of 2026-03-31 (proclamation applies to petitions filed at/after 2025-09-21 12:01 a.m. ET); USCIS H-1B Specialty Occupations page proclamation section
rules.notes: CONFIRMED from USCIS primary pages (retrieved 2026-07-06): the 2026-03-31 selection alert states the proclamation 'requires certain H-1B petitions filed at or after 12:01 a.m. Eastern on Sept. 21, 2025, to be accompanied by an additional $100,000 payment as a condition of eligibility,' and the USCIS H-1B page applies this to petitions requesting consular notification, port-of-entry notification, or pre-flight inspection. FY2027 cap petitions filed as in-US change of status are not subject if the change of status is granted.

rules.value: Petitions by institutions of higher education, nonprofit entities related to or affiliated with such institutions, nonprofit research organizations, and governmental research organizations are exempt from the annual H-1B caps and may be filed year-round with any start date; beneficiaries counted against the cap in the prior 6 years generally are not re-counted.
rules.notes: Definitional row for platform routing: cap-exempt filings bypass the registration/lottery process entirely.

UNVERIFIED: effective_date approximates the Immigration Act of 1990 H-1B/LCA implementation date and needs counsel confirmation; the requirement itself is verified current in eCFR (2026-07-06). Every H-1B I-129 (cap, transfer, extension, amendment) must include a copy of the signed DOL-certified Form ETA-9035/9035E for the worksite(s), reaffirming the employer's LCA attestation obligations. Referenced by transitions.json preconditions (rule_ref h1b-certified-lca-required).

An employer may submit only one registration per beneficiary per fiscal year; duplicates invalidate all of that employer's registrations for the beneficiary. Since the FY2025 beneficiary-centric rule, multiple bona fide employers may still register the same beneficiary, but the beneficiary receives selection entries per the weighted rule regardless of registration count.
